items.title: investingLive European FX news wrap: Markets consolidate on US Juneteenth
items.summary: How have interest rate expectations changed after this week's events? ECB policymaker Wunsch says upside surprises in inflation would justify further tightening ECB policymaker Lane sees the current inflation shock as "mid-sized", defends rate hike USD/JPY is near the highest level since 1986 as divergence with the Fed intensifies UK May retail sales +1.2% vs +0.5% m/m expected FX option expiries for 19 June 10am New York cut What are the main events for today? It's been a very light session with no major data or news releases. The markets were also pretty calm with things expected to die down further during the NA session due to the US holiday. As a reminder, the US stock market and bond market will be closed and will resume trading on Monday. Futures markets typically halt or experience early closures, often closing at 12:00 p.m. ET. The only economic data we got was the UK Retail Sales report. The data surprised to the upside across the board but the market reaction was pretty much muted as it doesn't change anything for the BoE. ECB policymakers Lane and Wunsch defended last week's rate hike decision arguing that the ECB had not made a mistake by hiking while inflation was rising and uncertainty remained elevated. Wunsch, in particular, acknowledged that easing geopolitical tensions involving Iran and moderating wage growth could strengthen the argument that the ECB might have been able to look through the energy-driven inflation spike. Still, he maintained that last week’s decision was justified given the information available at the time. In the American session, there's just the Canadian Retail Sales report which is not going to change anything for the BoC, so the market reaction will likely be muted. Have a great weekend! This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 本周事件发生后，市场对利率的预期发生了哪些变化？欧洲央行决策者温施表示，通胀的上行意外将为进一步收紧货币政策提供依据；欧洲央行决策者莱恩则认为当前的通胀冲击属于“中等规模”， 并为加息辩护；随着与美联储的政策分化加剧，美元兑日元汇率逼近1986年以来的最高水平；英国5月零售销售环比增长1.2%，高于预期值0.5%；6月19日纽约时间上午10点外汇期权到期；今日有哪些主要事件？今日交易时段相当清淡，没有重大数据或新闻发布。市场也相当平静，由于美国假期，预计北美交易时段市场将进一步趋于平静。提醒一下，美国股市和债市将休市，并将于周一恢复交易。期货市场通常会暂停交易或提前收盘，往往在美东时间中午12:00收盘。我们收到的唯一经济数据是英国零售销售报告。该数据全面好于预期，但市场反应相当平淡，因为这不会对英国央行（BoE）的政策产生任何影响。 欧洲央行决策者莱恩（Lane）和温施（Wunsch）为上周的加息决定进行了辩护，他们认为，在通胀持续攀升且不确定性依然居高不下的情况下，欧洲央行选择加息并未犯错。温施特别指出，伊朗相关地缘政治紧张局势的缓和以及工资增长放缓，或许能进一步佐证欧洲央行本可以忽略由能源价格推动的通胀飙升这一观点。尽管如此，他仍坚持认为，根据当时掌握的信息，上周的决定是合理的。在美股交易时段，仅公布加拿大零售销售报告，这不会对加拿大央行（BoC）的政策产生任何影响，因此市场反应预计将较为温和。祝大家周末愉快！本文作者为investinglive.com的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔（Giuseppe Dellamotta）。

items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have anything on the agenda so the price action will likely remain rangebound or the pullbacks that started during the Asian session extend further into the American session. We might also hear from a couple of ECB policymakers but the consensus is now to hold rates steady in July and delivering another rate hike in September if needed. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, we get the Canadian CPI report. The most important metric is the Trimmed-Mean CPI Y/Y which is expected to remain unchanged at 2.0%. The BoC left interest rates unchanged as expected at the last meeting and stated that it was seeing limited evidence that higher energy prices were fuelling broad-based inflation. The BoC said that it will continue to look through the short-term impact of energy price shock on headline inflation but will not let higher energy prices become persistent inflation. So, if higher energy prices lead to generalized inflation they may consider "consecutive rises in the policy rate". The market is pricing in just 21 bps of tightening this year which translates in a 66% chance of a rate hike in December at the earliest. CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS: 09:00 GMT/05:00 ET - ECB's Kocher (neutral - voter) 13:00 GMT/09:00 ET - ECB President Lagarde (neutral - voter) 13:00 GMT/09:00 ET - Fed's Waller (neutral - voter) 17:15 GMT/13:15 ET - ECB President Lagarde (neutral - voter) This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 在欧洲时段，没有重要事件安排，因此价格走势可能维持区间震荡，或者亚洲时段开始的回调将延续至美洲时段。我们可能会听到几位欧洲央行决策者的讲话，但目前市场普遍预期7月将维持利率不变，如有必要，9月将再次加息。 美洲时段 在美洲时段，我们将迎来加拿大CPI报告。最重要的指标是剔除极端值的CPI同比数据，预计将维持在2.0%不变。加拿大央行在上次会议上如预期般维持利率不变，并表示目前尚无充分证据表明能源价格上涨正在推动全面通胀。加拿大央行表示，将继续忽略能源价格冲击对整体通胀的短期影响，但不会允许能源价格上涨演变为持续性通胀。因此，如果能源价格上涨导致普遍性通胀，他们可能会考虑“连续上调政策利率”。市场目前仅定价今年加息21个基点，这意味着最早12月加息的概率为66%。 央行发言人：09:00 GMT/05:00 ET - 欧洲央行委员科赫（中性立场 - 有投票权） 13:00 GMT/09:00 ET - 欧洲央行行长拉加德（中性立场 - 有投票权） 13:00 GMT/09:00 ET - 美联储的沃勒（中性——有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间 17:15/东部时间 13:15 - 欧洲央行行长拉加德（中性——有投票权） 本文由 investinglive.com 的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔撰写。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Encouraging US-Iran progress weighs on oil, USD
items.summary: China bans rare earth exports to Pentagon-backed US firms in military-linked retaliation Explosion at Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG site leaves 54 injured and 18 missing Goldman Sachs sees structural gold buying as central bank demand hits 59 tonnes in April Recap: Japan PM Takaichi urges policy restraint after hawkish BOJ testimony on price risks Mediators map out 60-day roadmap for final US-Iran deal after breakthrough talks PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8150 (vs. estimate at 6.7733) Hormuz maritime security mechanism agreed by Iran in Switzerland breakthrough BOJ Dep Gov Himino warned delaying policy adjustments could trigger a inflation overshoot Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Himono to speak imminently Hormuz traffic plunges to five ships as Iran reasserts closure threat UK media (Guardian) reports Starmer set to confirm autumn departure on Monday, UK time US official says in-depth discussions held, working to ensure Hormuz remains open New York Times: China set to keep shaping oil prices as it holds back on imports ICYMI - Woman killed after Tesla using driver-assist system crashes into Texas home US futures - Globex - has opened for the new week, equity indexes down, oil up Politics weigh on GBP: Starmer given until Tuesday to set exit date or face mass resigns Qatar moves to contain Ras Laffan blast as questions linger over cause AXIOS report that US and Iran make headway on Hormuz access ahead of technical-level talks Monday open indicative forex prices, 22 June 2026 J.D. Vance: These kinds of ceasefires are awlays "a littel bit messy" Newsquawk Week in Focus: US PCE, Canadian CPI, EZ Flash PMI, Japanese Tokyo CPI Iran military command says it's closing the Strait of Hormuz due to ceasefire violations Session wrap — bullet summary Strait of Hormuz traffic collapsed to five vessels on Sunday after Iran announced a closure, down from 26 the previous day, before news flow improved as US-Iran talks in Switzerland continued despite an Iranian delegation walkout Qatar and Pakistan, as mediators at the Lake Lucerne summit, said encouraging progress was made across 18 hours of negotiations; the US and Iran agreed a 60-day roadmap to a final deal, a High Level Committee for political oversight, and working groups on nuclear, sanctions, and dispute resolution A communication line on Hormuz was established to ensure safe commercial passage for as long as talks continue; a de-confliction cell involving the US, Iran, Lebanon, and the mediators was also agreed to oversee the end of military operations in Lebanon Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi declared the blockade lifted, oil and petrochemical exports waived, some frozen assets to be released, and a major reconstruction and development plan for Iran in motion — though Israel was absent from the joint statement, raising questions about ceasefire durability Eighteen workers remain missing and at least 54 are injured after a large explosion at the Ras Laffan LNG plant near Doha, attributed to a technical accident during restart operations China banned dual-use exports to 10 US military-linked entities including rare earth miners MP Materials and USA Rare Earth, and barred Chinese buyers from procuring goods from 46 other US firms China held benchmark lending rates steady for a 13th consecutive month, with the one-year LPR at 3.00% and the five-year at 3.50%, signalling no urgency to ease despite sluggish credit growth The USD traded choppily and oil swung sharply, initially spiking on Iran's Hormuz closure announcement before reversing into negative territory as the Swiss talks produced cautious optimism A woman was killed in the US late Friday when a Tesla reportedly operating in driver-assist mode left its lane and struck a brick building, the woman's home, at high speed President Trump is scheduled to sign executive orders at 15:30 EDT / 20:30 BST Monday A session dominated by the push-pull of the US-Iran endgame, with the Strait of Hormuz once again the fulcrum. The new trading week opened badly, with Hormuz traffic cratering to five vessels on Sudnay after Iran announced a closure and its delegation walked out of the Lake Lucerne talks in protest at Trump's online threats to resume bombing. Oil spiked, the dollar churned, and for a few hours last week's fragile optimism looked like it might not survive contact with Monday. It didn't hold as a bearish story. The joint statement from Qatari and Pakistani mediators carried enough weight to reverse the move; 18 hours of talks, a 60-day roadmap, a High Level Committee, working groups across nuclear, sanctions, and dispute resolution, and crucially a dedicated communication line on Hormuz designed to prevent exactly the kind of incident that nearly derailed the day. Iranian FM Araghchi went further than the joint statement, declaring the blockade lifted, frozen assets moving, and a reconstruction plan for Iran underway. Oil slipped back into the red. The absence of Israel from the joint statement remains the most conspicuous gap. The de-confliction cell for Lebanon includes the US, Iran, and the mediators, but a durable ceasefire that doesn't explicitly bind Israel is a framework with an obvious pressure point. Away from the main event, Ras Laffan provided a sharp reminder that the physical infrastructure underpinning Gulf energy is still fragile; 18 missing, 54 injured in an explosion during restart operations, with QatarEnergy still silent on plant damage. China's rare earth export controls added another layer, targeting the Pentagon-backed MP Materials and USA Rare Earth in a move that goes well beyond symbolism. Beijing also held its lending rates for a 13th straight month, underscoring that whatever stimulus is coming, it isn't coming via the rate channel. Trump will be signing executive orders land at 15:30 EDT. Given the day's trajectory, the market will be watching for any ill-dsiciplined Iran-related comments from him. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 中国以军事相关报复为由，禁止向五角大楼支持的美国企业出口稀土 卡塔尔拉斯拉凡液化天然气厂发生爆炸，造成54人受伤、18人失踪 高盛认为黄金出现结构性买盘，4月央行需求达59吨 回顾： 日本首相高市早苗在日央行就通胀风险发表鹰派证词后呼吁政策克制 调解方在突破性会谈后为美伊最终协议制定60天路线图 中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8150（较 （估计为6.7733） 伊朗在瑞士就霍尔木兹海峡海上安全机制达成协议，取得突破 日本央行副行长日野野警告称，推迟政策调整可能引发通胀超调 日本央行副行长日野野即将发表讲话 伊朗重申封锁威胁，霍尔木兹海峡船只通行量骤降至5艘 英国媒体（《卫报》）报道，斯塔默预计将于英国时间周一确认秋季离任 美国官员称已举行深入讨论，正努力确保霍尔木兹海峡保持畅通 《纽约时报》： 中国将通过限制进口继续影响油价 值得关注——特斯拉启用辅助驾驶系统后撞入得州民宅致一女子死亡 美国期货——Globex——已开启新一周交易，股指下跌，油价上涨 政治因素拖累英镑： 斯塔默须在周二前确定离任日期，否则将面临大规模辞职 卡塔尔采取措施控制拉斯拉凡爆炸事件，但起因仍存疑问 AXIOS报道称，在周一技术层级会谈前，美国和伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡通行问题上取得进展 2026年6月22日周一开盘外汇参考价格 J.D. 万斯：此类停火协议总是“有点乱” Newsquawk 本周焦点：美国个人消费支出（PCE）、加拿大消费者物价指数（CPI）、欧元区初步采购经理人指数（PMI）， 日本东京CPI 伊朗军事指挥部称因停火协议遭违反将封锁霍尔木兹海峡 盘点——要点摘要 周日，伊朗宣布封锁霍尔木兹海峡后，该海峡船只通行量骤降至5艘，较前一日的26艘大幅减少；随后随着瑞士美伊谈判的持续（尽管伊朗代表团曾一度退场），市场情绪有所好转。作为卢塞恩湖峰会调解方的卡塔尔和巴基斯坦表示，在长达18小时的谈判中取得了令人鼓舞的进展； 美伊双方就达成最终协议的60天路线图、设立政治监督高级别委员会，以及组建核问题、制裁和争端解决工作组达成一致；为确保谈判期间商业船只的安全通行，双方建立了霍尔木兹海峡沟通渠道； 各方还同意成立一个由美国、伊朗、黎巴嫩及调解方组成的冲突化解小组，以监督在黎巴嫩结束军事行动。伊朗外长阿拉格奇宣布，封锁已解除，石油和石化产品出口禁令已取消，部分冻结资产将被解冻，并进行重大重 伊朗的重建与发展计划已启动——尽管以色列未出现在联合声明中，这令人对停火的持久性产生质疑。多哈附近的拉斯拉凡液化天然气厂发生大规模爆炸，据称是重启作业期间发生的技术事故，目前仍有18名工人失踪，至少54人受伤。中国禁止向10家与美国军方有关联的实体出口两用物品，其中包括稀土开采企业MP Materials和USA Rare Earth， 并禁止中国买家从另外46家美国企业采购商品。中国连续第13个月维持基准贷款利率不变，一年期LPR为3.00%，五年期为3.50%，这表明尽管信贷增长乏力，但当局尚无放松政策的紧迫感。美元汇率波动剧烈，油价剧烈震荡， 起初因伊朗宣布封锁霍尔木兹海峡而飙升，随后随着瑞士谈判传出谨慎乐观的消息，油价转而跌入负值区域。上周五晚间，美国一名女子遇难，据报道，一辆处于驾驶辅助模式的特斯拉偏离车道，以高速撞上了砖砌建筑——即该女子的住所。特朗普总统定于周一东部夏令时15:30/英国夏令时20:30签署行政命令。本交易日主要受推-p 美伊局势已进入收官阶段，霍尔木兹海峡再次成为关键支点。新的一周交易开局不利：周日，在伊朗宣布封锁海峡并因其代表团抗议特朗普在网上威胁重启轰炸而退出卢塞恩湖会谈后，霍尔木兹海峡的船只通行量骤降至仅5艘。油价飙升，美元剧烈震荡，上周那份脆弱的乐观情绪在周一的冲击下一度似乎难以维系。但这一看跌走势并未持续。卡塔尔和巴基斯坦调解员发表的联合声明具有足够分量，扭转了这一走势；18小时的谈判、一份60天的路线图、一个高级别委员会、涵盖核问题、制裁及争端解决各领域的工作组，以及至关重要的是，一条专门针对霍尔木兹海峡的沟通热线——其目的正是为了防止当天险些导致谈判破裂的那类事件再次发生。伊朗外长阿拉格奇的表态比联合声明更进一步，宣布封锁已解除、被冻结资产正在解冻，且针对伊朗的重建计划已启动。油价随即再度跌入负值区间。以色列未被列入联合声明，仍是其中最显眼的缺失。 黎巴嫩冲突化解工作组包括美国、伊朗以及调解方，但一项未明确约束以色列的持久停火协议，其框架中存在一个显而易见的薄弱环节。在主要事件之外，拉斯拉凡事件有力地提醒人们，支撑海湾地区能源的实体基础设施依然脆弱；在重启作业期间发生的爆炸中，有18人失踪、54人受伤，而卡塔尔能源公司至今仍未就工厂受损情况发表声明。中国对稀土出口的管控又增添了一层复杂性，其针对五角大楼支持的MP Materials和USA Rare Earth公司的举措，其意义远不止于象征意义。北京方面还连续第13个月维持贷款利率不变，这表明无论未来推出何种刺激措施，都不会通过利率渠道实施。特朗普将于美国东部时间15:30签署行政命令。鉴于今日的走势，市场将密切关注他是否会发表任何关于伊朗的不当言论。本文作者为investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan。

Vance: These kinds of ceasefires are awlays "a littel bit messy" Newsquawk Week in Focus: US PCE, Canadian CPI, EZ Flash PMI, Japanese Tokyo CPI Iran military command says it's closing the Strait of Hormuz due to ceasefire violations Session wrap — bullet summary Strait of Hormuz traffic collapsed to five vessels on Sunday after Iran announced a closure, down from 26 the previous day, before news flow improved as US-Iran talks in Switzerland continued despite an Iranian delegation walkout Qatar and Pakistan, as mediators at the Lake Lucerne summit, said encouraging progress was made across 18 hours of negotiations; the US and Iran agreed a 60-day roadmap to a final deal, a High Level Committee for political oversight, and working groups on nuclear, sanctions, and dispute resolution A communication line on Hormuz was established to ensure safe commercial passage for as long as talks continue; a de-confliction cell involving the US, Iran, Lebanon, and the mediators was also agreed to oversee the end of military operations in Lebanon Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi declared the blockade lifted, oil and petrochemical exports waived, some frozen assets to be released, and a major reconstruction and development plan for Iran in motion — though Israel was absent from the joint statement, raising questions about ceasefire durability Eighteen workers remain missing and at least 54 are injured after a large explosion at the Ras Laffan LNG plant near Doha, attributed to a technical accident during restart operations China banned dual-use exports to 10 US military-linked entities including rare earth miners MP Materials and USA Rare Earth, and barred Chinese buyers from procuring goods from 46 other US firms China held benchmark lending rates steady for a 13th consecutive month, with the one-year LPR at 3.00% and the five-year at 3.50%, signalling no urgency to ease despite sluggish credit growth The USD traded choppily and oil swung sharply, initially spiking on Iran's Hormuz closure announcement before reversing into negative territory as the Swiss talks produced cautious optimism A woman was killed in the US late Friday when a Tesla reportedly operating in driver-assist mode left its lane and struck a brick building, the woman's home, at high speed President Trump is scheduled to sign executive orders at 15:30 EDT / 20:30 BST Monday A session dominated by the push-pull of the US-Iran endgame, with the Strait of Hormuz once again the fulcrum. The new trading week opened badly, with Hormuz traffic cratering to five vessels on Sudnay after Iran announced a closure and its delegation walked out of the Lake Lucerne talks in protest at Trump's online threats to resume bombing. Oil spiked, the dollar churned, and for a few hours last week's fragile optimism looked like it might not survive contact with Monday. It didn't hold as a bearish story. The joint statement from Qatari and Pakistani mediators carried enough weight to reverse the move; 18 hours of talks, a 60-day roadmap, a High Level Committee, working groups across nuclear, sanctions, and dispute resolution, and crucially a dedicated communication line on Hormuz designed to prevent exactly the kind of incident that nearly derailed the day. Iranian FM Araghchi went further than the joint statement, declaring the blockade lifted, frozen assets moving, and a reconstruction plan for Iran underway. Oil slipped back into the red. The absence of Israel from the joint statement remains the most conspicuous gap. The de-confliction cell for Lebanon includes the US, Iran, and the mediators, but a durable ceasefire that doesn't explicitly bind Israel is a framework with an obvious pressure point. Away from the main event, Ras Laffan provided a sharp reminder that the physical infrastructure underpinning Gulf energy is still fragile; 18 missing, 54 injured in an explosion during restart operations, with QatarEnergy still silent on plant damage. China's rare earth export controls added another layer, targeting the Pentagon-backed MP Materials and USA Rare Earth in a move that goes well beyond symbolism. Beijing also held its lending rates for a 13th straight month, underscoring that whatever stimulus is coming, it isn't coming via the rate channel. Trump will be signing executive orders land at 15:30 EDT. Given the day's trajectory, the market will be watching for any ill-dsiciplined Iran-related comments from him. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
